Japanese: 同日　某時刻 警察署・刑事課
English: January 7 Police Station Criminal Affairs Dept.

Japanese: イトノコさん、いないね。
English: Looks like Gumshoe's not here.

Japanese: それより‥‥なんか、 いつもとフンイキがちがうぞ。
English: Never mind that. What's going on? It feels different in here somehow...

Japanese: あれ。そうかな？
English: You think?

Japanese: ミョーに殺気立ってるというか‥‥
English: Yeah. Everyone seems to be on edge.

Japanese: 何してるんだ！　聞き込みの 人員を、はやくまわしてくれ！
English: What are you doing!? Call in the officers for the briefing, quick!

Japanese: 署のサーバーマシン、 シャットダウンできないのか？
English: Can't you shut down the station's server?

Japanese: 課長！　インターネットは やめてください！
English: Chief! Quit playing on the Internet!

Japanese: いや、しかし、メールトモダチの サヨコさんがだね。
English: But my e-mail pen pal, 1337aZnPrInceSz!

Japanese: また今度にしてください！ はい。スイッチ、切りますよ！
English: Save it for later! I'm turning it off NOW!

Japanese: ぎゃー！　サヨコさん‥‥
English: Noooo! 1337aZnPrInceeeeeSz!!

Japanese: ‥‥みんな、なんか イキイキしているね。
English: Everyone's keeping busy in here, huh?

Japanese: “バタバタしている”と言った ほうが正しいんじゃないかな。
English: Keeping busy? More like panicking, if you ask me.

Japanese: （何か、事件が起こったらしいな ‥‥それも、重大な‥‥）
English: (Something's going on. Something big...)

Japanese: あ、カワイイ！　これって、 ケーサツのマスコットだよね。
English: Hey, that's the police mascot, isn't it? It's so cute!

Japanese: “タイホくん”だよ。
English: That's the "Blue Badger".

Japanese: ワシが考えて、ワシが作った。 今や、この国の警察のシンボルさ。
English: It was my idea! I made it! And now it's the national symbol of the police!

Japanese: １ぴき、ふえてますね。 ‥‥ピンクのが。
English: So what's with the pink one? It's new, right?

Japanese: “１ぴき”って言わない、 なるほどくん。
English: She's not an "it", Nick. She's a "she"! Right, Chief!?

Japanese: それは、新しい仲間の “タイホちゃん”だよ。
English: Yup. Meet the "Pink Badger".

Japanese: あれ。 名前、おんなじなんですか？
English: So one's called "Blue", and the other's called "Pink", but they're both called "Badger"?

Japanese: ケッコンしてるからね、 その２人は。
English: You got it! They're married.

Japanese: あの。タイホくんとタイホちゃん ‥‥手錠でつながれてますけど。
English: Um... Mr. & Mrs. Badger seem to be cuffed together, Chief.

Japanese: ‥‥それが ケッコンというヤツだからねえ。
English: You got it! That's marriage for you!

Japanese: （さすが警察のマスコット‥‥ 重たいな、なんとなく）
English: (Whoa... A mascot that's deeper than the deepest of deep oceans...)

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “ひったくり　ひったくられて いたみわけ”
English: "Even pickpockets can have their pockets picked."

Japanese: ‥‥キマリだな。一見、 標語っぽくないのがイイ。
English: That's a keeper! Better to go with something that doesn't sound too much like a slogan.

Japanese: 防犯キャンペーンの 標語を考えている‥‥
English: He must be coming up with slogans for a crime prevention campaign...

Japanese: ようには、とても見えない。
English: ...But I'm not sure even he knows what kind of crime he's trying to prevent.

Japanese: 刑事課の課長さんだろう。
English: This must be the chief of the detectives here.

Japanese: ‥‥パソコンのスイッチを 切られて、ボーゼンとしている。
English: He looks lost, now that the power to his computer has been cut.

Japanese: ‥‥しかたがない。 気分を変えて、手紙にするか。
English: Oh, well. I guess I'll just have to write her a real letter instead of an email.

Japanese: （捜査しろよ‥‥）
English: (Alternately, you could write up some reports... Just a suggestion.)

Japanese: 『サヨコさん、お元気ですか。 ぼくは元気です』‥‥と。
English: "Dear 1337aZnPrInceSz, How are you? I'm OK! How was the show last night...?"

Japanese: ‥‥あたし、カチョーに なりたくなってきたよ。
English: Wow, what an awesome job! Maybe I should send in my resume and become chief!

Japanese: 婦人警官がビシッと 敬礼している。
English: A poster of a female police officer...

Japanese: ‥‥と思ったら、制服を着た アイドルのグラビアだった。
English: Wait, no. That's the latest "Babes in Uniform" calendar. My bad.

Japanese: 刑事たちの机がならんでいる。
English: These are the detectives' desks.

Japanese: パソコンに、資料。 ‥‥思ったより片づいているな。
English: There are computers and files on each one. Funny, they're a lot tidier than I expected.

Japanese: たぶん、あまり席にいることが ないんだろう。
English: I guess the detectives don't spend a lot of time at their desks.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 警察署・刑事課
English: January 7 Police Station Criminal Affairs Dept.

Japanese: 署のマシンがパンクしました！
English: The main server just went up in smoke!

Japanese: 記者会見の用意はまだか！ 総監が見えたぞ！
English: Why the heck isn't the press conference set up yet!? The Superintendent's here already!

Japanese: ううん。インターネットでも 話題になっているようだな。
English: Yeah. And there's a problem with the Internet, too.

Japanese: だから！　課長、コンピュータを 使わないでください！
English: I already told you to stop using your computer, Chief!

Japanese: いや、しかし今、ワシの王手が‥‥
English: But I'm watching videos online! I'm catching up on my Asian soap operas!

Japanese: また今度にしてください！ はい。スイッチ、切りますよ！
English: It's gonna have to wait, Chief. I'm throwing the switch!

Japanese: ぎゃー！　ワシの王手が‥‥
English: Nooo! Just when Sum Yung-Gai was about to confess to his son's hot-to-trot girlfriend!

Japanese: いやー、 盛り上がってるねえ。
English: Wow! This place is really buzzing.

Japanese: 何か事件が起こったみたいだな。 ‥‥とびきり、ハデなのが。
English: Something must be going down. Something really big.

Japanese: おッ！　アンタたち！
English: Huh!? What are you doing here, pal?

Japanese: あ、イトノコ刑事さん！
English: Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: ダメッスよ。 こんなときに来ちゃあ。
English: You can't be here right now.

Japanese: アンタたちまでかり出されるッス。 ‥‥聞き込みに。
English: You'll be roped into the briefing if you stay.

Japanese: ま、まさか。
English: Huh?

Japanese: とにかく、タイヘンな コトになってるッス！
English: We've got big problems here today.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。何があったんですか？
English: Why? What's going on?

Japanese: ウイルスッス！　ウイルスッス！
English: It's a virus! A virus!!

Japanese: （ウイルス‥‥？）
English: (A virus?)

Japanese: コンピュータ・ウイルスが 署内で暴れ狂っているッス！
English: There's a virus ripping through the precinct's computer system.

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥ いろいろ聞くことがあるのに）
English: (But I really need to ask you some questions...)

Japanese: ‥‥あああああッ、そおッスゥッ！
English: Aaaaargh! I can't believe it!

Japanese: イチバン重要なコトを 忘れていたッス！
English: I almost forgot the most important thing!

Japanese: な、なんですか‥‥？
English: A-And that is...?

Japanese: 決まってるじゃないッスかッ！ ‥‥ベントウッスよ！
English: You know, the lunchbox! How did everything go!?

Japanese: ベントウ‥‥？
English: L-Lunchbox?

Japanese: あれだよ。ウインナーの‥‥
English: You remember. The weenies...?

Japanese: スズキ、ウインナーは 大ッキライッス！
English: I hate weenies!

Japanese: （‥‥ああ、あれか‥‥）
English: (Oh, yeah... Those weenies.)

Japanese: どうだったッスか！ 自分のベントウはッ！
English: So? How did my weenies taste when they went down the hatch?

Japanese: え！　えーと‥‥あの。 ‥‥‥‥‥‥おいしかったです。
English: Huh? Um... Well... It was... delicious!

Japanese: そーッスか！　そーッスか！ そりゃ、よかったッス！
English: Yeah? That's what she said? Really!?

Japanese: あ、いや。そういう イミじゃなくて‥‥
English: Umm... Well... Not exactly...

Japanese: ダイジョーブ！ シンパイ、いらないッス！
English: Don't worry about it, pal.

Japanese: そんなコトもあろうかと‥‥ ちゃーんと、作っておいたッス！
English: I figured something would happen, so I came prepared.

Japanese: え‥‥ な、ナニを‥‥？
English: Prepared? What do you mean...?

Japanese: おかわりの大盛りベントウッス！
English: I made a jumbo lunchbox!

Japanese: は‥‥はああ‥‥。
English: Oh...

Japanese: じゃ、またコレ。 よろしくたのむッス。
English: Do me a favor again, huh, pal, and deliver this?

Japanese: （ズッシリ重いな‥‥ あらゆるイミで‥‥）
English: (This sure is a heavy burden... In more ways than one...)

Japanese: マコクンのよろこぶカオが 目に浮かぶッス！
English: I can just imagine Maggey's little eyes sparkling with joy when you bring her that!

Japanese: また食べるの？　ウインナー‥‥
English: ...Weenies, again, Nick? Tell me we don't have to eat all these too...

Japanese: 《イトノコのベントウ》を ふたたび、真宵ちゃんに持たせた。
English: Gumshoe's Lunchbox given to Maya. Again.

Japanese: もう食べられないよ、あたし。
English: I really can't eat any more...

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “カギしめて　元センしめて 抱きしめて”‥‥
English: "Your batteries won't last forever. Remember that when you're having fun... in bed."

Japanese: ‥‥キマリだな。さりげなく 火遊びを風刺しているのがイイ。
English: We have a winner! A little joke to make people think about their smoke alarms.

Japanese: 防火キャンペーンの 標語を考えている‥‥
English: He must be coming up with slogans for a fire safety campaign...

Japanese: つもりなんだろうな、 あくまでも本人としては。
English: ...I guess? ...Does he even know what he's trying to get across anymore?

Japanese: 刑事課の課長さんだろう。
English: This must be the chief of the detectives here.

Japanese: ‥‥パソコンのスイッチを 切られて、ボーゼンとしている。
English: He looks lost, now that the power to his computer has been cut.

Japanese: ‥‥しかたがない。 気分を変えて、新聞を読むか。
English: Oh, well. I guess I'll just have to read the paper now.

Japanese: （捜査しろよ‥‥）
English: (Alternately, you could read some reports... Just a suggestion.)

Japanese: ‥‥って、なんだ！ １ヶ月も前の古新聞じゃないか！
English: What the heck is this!? This paper's more than a month old!

Japanese: しかも、サインペンでラクガキ してあるし！
English: And someone's written all over it!

Japanese: こんなもの、ワシの机に置くなッ！ ‥‥イヤガラセか？
English: Who's the clown that put this on my desk? Well? Speak up!

Japanese: くしゃくしゃに丸めて 捨てちゃったよ。
English: He just wadded it up and chucked it in the trash!

Japanese: ‥‥ダイジな証拠品なのに。
English: But that's an important piece of evidence!

Japanese: あの‥‥なんですか？ コンピュータ・ウイルスって。
English: So what exactly is a computer virus, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: 知らねッス。
English: I dunno.

Japanese: え。
English: What?

Japanese: 自分は、このさわぎに ただ、オドらされているだけッス。
English: Look, I just go with the flow, alright, pal?

Japanese: （あきれた刑事さんだな‥‥）
English: (And here I thought detectives were supposed to be somewhat knowledgeable...)

Japanese: あー！　なんスか、そのカオ！ じゃーアンタ、知ってるッスか！
English: What's with that face, pal? You think you know what a virus is?

Japanese: 知ってるッスか？　なるほどくん！
English: Well, Nick? Do you?

Japanese: コンピュータ・ウイルス、か。 まあ、カンタンなコトなら‥‥。
English: A computer virus? Sure. I mean, only in simple terms, of course.

Japanese: えッ！　スゴい、なるほどくん！ ものしりハカセだ！
English: Really? Wow, you know everything, Professor Nick!

Japanese: これから、ハカセって呼ぶッス！
English: Yeah. I'm gonna call you Dr. Wright from now on!

Japanese: あ。いいなそれ。 ‥‥ね？　ハカセ。
English: Hey, that sounds pretty cool! Don't you agree, Dr. Wright?

Japanese: （バカにされてるような気がする）
English: (Why do I get the feeling they're making fun of me?)

Japanese: しかたないな。あまり詳しく ないけど、説明しましょうか？
English: OK, fine. I'm no expert, but I can at least explain the basics to the two of you.

Japanese: “ウイルス”は、コンピュータを 破壊するためのプログラムです。
English: A virus is a program that gets inside a computer and causes damage.

Japanese: は、ハカイ‥‥って、 ボカーン！　と行くッスか！
English: Damage? You mean it makes the machine go "boom" and explode?

Japanese: ‥‥ちがいます。コワすのは‥‥ そう。コンピュータの中身、かな。
English: No. The damage is, um... Well, it's all internal.

Japanese: 中身がボカーン！　って行くの？
English: So the inside goes "boom", right?

Japanese: たとえば、警察のコンピュータには 事件のデータが記録されてるだろ？
English: Imagine all the case data you've got stored on your PCs here in the station...

Japanese: それが、ウイルスプログラムで ゼンブ、ぱあになるわけ。
English: A virus could wipe out all that. That's the kind of damage I'm talking about.

Japanese: ひゃあ。コワいねー、ウイルス。
English: Whoa! That's scary.

Japanese: もっとコワいのは、ウイルスは 伝染する、ってトコロだ。
English: Yeah. And what's even more scary is that viruses are infectious.

Japanese: でんせん‥‥？
English: Infectious?

Japanese: コンピュータは、ネットワークで つながってるコトが多いだろ？
English: Most computers are connected together on a network, right?

Japanese: ウイルスは、それらのマシンを 次々に破壊していくんだよ。
English: A virus can move from one machine to another over the network.

Japanese: ネットワークを通じて、ウイルスは 自分で勝手に、増えていくわけだ。
English: So the virus just keeps spreading faster and faster.

Japanese: うーん。ホンモノの 伝染病みたいッスねー。
English: Hmph. Just like a real virus, huh.

Japanese: でも、でも！　なんでそんな プログラムを作るのかな。
English: But, Nick! Why would anyone want to make a program like that?

Japanese: そッス。せっかく作ったデータを ぶちコワしたら、イミがないッス！
English: Yeah. It takes ages to type in all that data. Why would you wanna destroy it, pal?

Japanese: いやいや。ウイルスプログラムは、 他人のマシンに送り込むんです。
English: No. People don't infect their own machines. They send the virus to someone else's.

Japanese: うわ。ヒドいの！
English: What!? That's horrible!

Japanese: あー。つまりアレッスか。
English: Oh, I get it...

Japanese: アンタが、ゴドー検事にクシャミ 引っかけてカゼをうつす、という。
English: It's like you sneezing on Godot so he catches a cold.

Japanese: そうか！　それでゴドー検事は 法廷をお休み、と。
English: Right! Then he wouldn't be able to turn up in court because he'd be too sick.

Japanese: ‥‥あいかわらずワルだねー、 なるほどくん。
English: You really shouldn't do stuff like that, Nick. It's wrong.

Japanese: なんでぼくの話になるんだよ！
English: Who? What? Where? When and why did the conversation jump to talking about me!?

Japanese: とにかく、それがコンピュータ・ ウイルスだよ。悪いプログラムだ。
English: Anyway, that's what a computer virus is. A bad program that causes damage.

Japanese: ウイルスプログラムには、いちいち 名前がついているッス。
English: And all the different viruses have names, right?

Japanese: 今回、ウチで暴れてるウイルス‥‥ どこかで聞いた名前なんスがねえ。
English: I kinda feel like I've heard the name of the virus we caught somewhere before.

Japanese: （ウイルスプログラムの名前。 ココロ当たりがあるような‥‥）
English: (The name of the virus, huh? I feel like I've heard of it before, too...)

Japanese: 《クリーニング・ボンバー》の 情報は、以前から流れていたッス。
English: We already knew about the MC Bomber virus from a while back.

Japanese: 警察の上層部に、ナニやら 取引を持ちかけたグループが‥‥
English: A group of criminals issued a series of demands to the head honchos of law enforcement.

Japanese: 『取引に応じなければ、ウイルスを バラまくぞ』と脅してきたッス。
English: They threatened to release the virus if their demands weren't met.

Japanese: ダレですか？　そんなダイタンな コトをするの。
English: Who are they?

Japanese: ウラ社会の大物、らしいッスね。 ‥‥自分は知らねッス。
English: I dunno. Some hotshots from the criminal underworld would be my guess.

Japanese: そのウイルスが、送りこまれて きた、というわけですか‥‥。
English: And now the virus has been released, huh...

Japanese: 公共機関のコンピュータが、 バクハツ的に感染して‥‥
English: Yeah. It's in every computer in every public office in the city.

Japanese: もお、お祭りさわぎッス！ カーニバルッス！
English: Everyone's going nuts! They're hopping around like they're dancing at a carnival!

Japanese: すごいなあ。なんか、アレ。 “えすえふ”みたいですよねー。
English: All this stuff with criminals and viruses... It almost feels like we're in a sci-fi movie!

Japanese: かなりウデのいいプログラマーが 作ったウイルスらしいッス。
English: Apparently the programmer who made the virus was a real genius or something.

Japanese: 今、コイツが売りさばかれた 闇ルートを、必死で探してるッス！
English: The focus right now is on tracing the route of this virus on the black market.

Japanese: ウイルスプログラム‥‥って、 “商品”になるんですか？
English: You mean someone put this virus up for sale?

Japanese: そうみたいッス。特に、今回の ウイルスはよくできてるッスから、
English: Yeah. And because this one's so powerful...

Japanese: “億”単位のカネが 動いていると思われるッス。
English: ...they're estimating its price tag was in the millions of dollars, pal.

Japanese: （ウイルスプログラムに、そんな 価値があったなんて‥‥！）
English: (In the millions!? A virus can be worth that much!?)

Japanese: 《クリーニング・ボンバー》の データを書きなおした。
English: MC Bomber updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: いいッスか。よく聞くッス！
English: OK, I'm only gonna say this once, so listen up!

Japanese: ‥‥は、はい！
English: Y-Yes!

Japanese: いくらビンボーでも、こういう ところから借りちゃダメッス！
English: No matter how poor you get, never borrow money from a place like this, you hear?

Japanese: ‥‥は、はい？
そういうときは‥‥ ソーメンを食べて、ガマンッス！
English: If you got money trouble, just go on a diet of instant noodles and hang in there!

Japanese: いやいや！　借金じゃなくて、 この会社の情報がほしいんですよ！
English: Ah, we're not thinking about borrowing money, Detective! We want information.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ。
English: Oh... Is that all? Well, let's see...

Japanese: カリヨーゼは、闇金融の中でも 特にアヤシイ会社ッス。
English: Tender Lender is considered to be even fishier than the average illegal loan shark.

Japanese: なんか最近、急に カネに困りだしたらしく‥‥、
English: And it seems it ran into trouble just recently.

Japanese: かなりゴーインな 取り立てをしている、とか。
English: Those guys have been pretty heavy-handed, calling in all their debts.

Japanese: そうなんですか‥‥
English: Really?

Japanese: あの会社には、クビを つっこまないほうがいいッス。
English: Don't go poking your nose around in their business, pal.

Japanese: 彼女にニラまれたら、 ブジじゃすまないッスからねえ。
English: You'll really regret it if you upset that lady.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 （‥‥って、ちょっと待て！）
English: Alright, I get the picture. (Hey, wait a minute!)

Japanese: （今、なんて言った！ ‥‥“彼女”‥‥？）
English: (What did he just say!? "That lady"?)

Japanese: ‥‥ダレのことかな。
English: Who's this lady he's talking about, Nick?

Japanese: （詳しく聞いてみるか‥‥。 “彼女”のこと‥‥）
English: (We better find out what the story is with this "lady"...)

Japanese: あまり聞きたくないですけど‥‥ 《鹿羽組》というのは？
English: I'm not sure if I really want to get involved in this, but who are the Cadaverinis?

Japanese: まー、コワい連中ッスな。
English: Who are they? A scary bunch of people, that's who!

Japanese: （刑事のクセに、“コワい” ってのも、どうだろう‥‥）
English: (You're a cop, and you're scared? What's that about?)

Japanese: 刑事だろうと小学生だろうと、 コワいものはコワいッス！
English: Trust me, it doesn't matter if you're a kid or a cop, these guys are scary.

Japanese: ウラ社会で絶大な力を持つ、 やっかいなグループッス。
English: They've got some serious clout in the criminal underworld.

Japanese: とにかく連中、ミョーに資金が あるので、手に負えないッス。
English: We can't touch them. They've got too much moola.

Japanese: 資金が‥‥？
English: Moola...? As in?

Japanese: 街の闇金融業界をひとまとめに して、かせいでいるとか。
English: They pretty much control all the cash on the city's black market.

Japanese: 闇金融‥‥というと、 《カリヨーゼ》も‥‥？
English: The black market, huh? And that includes Tender Lender, I take it?

Japanese: トーゼン、鹿羽　権太には アタマが上がらないッス。
English: Sure. No one stands up to Bruto Cadaverini. And I mean, no one!

Japanese: （そうだったのか‥‥）
English: (Interesting...)

Japanese: うらみさん、そんなコワい人の おマゴさんだったんですね‥‥
English: So Viola's the granddaughter of some mafia boss then?

Japanese: 鹿羽　権太のマゴ好きは 有名ッス。
English: Yeah. And everyone knows how much Bruto loves his little girl.

Japanese: たったヒトリのマゴで、 組長の生きがいッス。
English: She means everything to him.

Japanese: そういえば‥‥
English: So...

Japanese: どうして彼女、 《カリヨーゼ》にいるんですか？
English: How did she end up at Tender Lender?

Japanese: さー。そこまでは 知らないッスけど‥‥。
English: I dunno, pal.

Japanese: でも、かなりアツアツな ふたりらしいッス。
English: But I heard she and the boss of the Tender Lender are pretty tight.

Japanese: あつあつ‥‥
English: Tight?

Japanese: マコクンの事件で、関係者の資料に そう書いてあったッス。
English: That's what it said in a file I read related to Maggey's case.

Japanese: （これは‥‥かなり重要な 手がかりだな‥‥）
English: (Sounds like a pretty important clue.)

Japanese: まちがいなく、マコクンに わたしてほしいッス！
English: Don't forget to give that to Maggey, OK?

Japanese: あ‥‥でも、留置所って 差し入れ、いけないんじゃあ‥‥？
English: But they don't allow presents for the prisoners in the detention center, right...?

Japanese: あれ！　そう言えば そうだったッスっけ。
English: Hey, you're right...

Japanese: なんにせよ、マコクンに わたしてほしいッス！
English: Well, it doesn't matter. I'm counting on you to get it to her somehow.

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥）
English: (I'm a lawyer, not a delivery boy, Gumshoe...)

Japanese: あの‥‥イトノコ刑事。 これ、なんですけど‥‥
English: Detective Gumshoe, umm... about this...

Japanese: なんスか！　自分は今、 マコクンとウイルスで、アタマが
English: What!? I'm trying to concentrate on Maggey and this virus right now, so I...

Japanese: うほおほおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: Aaaaaaaaaaah!!

Japanese: これッス！　この、 フザけた名前！　思い出したッス！
English: This is it! This stupid name! I remember now!

Japanese: （やっぱり、な‥‥）
English: (I thought so. Here it comes...)

Japanese: ナニよー！　ヒトリでうなずいて ないで、教えてよー！
English: Don't just nod to yourself and keep me in the dark, Nick! What's going on!?

Japanese: な、泣くなよ！
English: It's OK, Maya! You don't have to cry about it!

Japanese: スポーツ新聞のラクガキ‥‥ そして、ＣＤ‥‥
English: The name scribbled on that sports paper and written on that CD...

Japanese: 《クリーニング・ボンバー》 ‥‥ウイルスの名前ッス！
English: That's the name of the virus! "MC Bomber"!

Japanese: ええええええええええッ！
English: WHAT!?

Japanese: 今、署内でウワサの コンピュータ・ウイルスッス！
English: Yeah! The virus that's just infected every computer in the station, pal. It's MC Bomber!

Japanese: もう少し、詳しく 聞かせてもらえますか？
English: Can you give us any more details? Please!?

Japanese: あんまり、ヒトに見せないほうが いいッスよ、その写真。
English: I wouldn't go flashing that photo around if I were you, pal.

Japanese: どうしてですか？
English: Why not?

Japanese: なにぶん、ウラ社会の ボスッスからねー。
English: That guy rules the criminal underworld.

Japanese: こっぴどい目にあうッス。 ‥‥くわばらくわばら。
English: You could get yourselves in serious trouble. I'm talking say-your-prayers trouble.

Japanese: イトノコさん、ふるえてるよ。
English: You're shaking, Detective.

Japanese: 意外にヨワムシだな。
English: Like a leaf...

Japanese: 自分は、その。 ‥‥頭脳派ッスから。
English: I'm just... You know... Kinda on edge at the moment... If you know what I mean.

Japanese: コイツが、ゼニトラッスか‥‥。
English: Oh, this guy... The Tiger...

Japanese: 有名なんですか？
English: Is he famous?

Japanese: まさに、ゼニを貸すトラ みたいなオトコッス！
English: Yeah. This guy's not a loan shark, you know. Nope. He's a big loan cat!

Japanese: そのまんまですね。
English: Hence the name?

Japanese: 返さないと、食われるッス。 ‥‥くわばらくわばら。
English: Don't pay him back, and you'd better say your prayers, because he'll eat you alive.

Japanese: イトノコさん、ふるえてるよ。
English: You're shaking, Detective.

Japanese: 意外にヨワムシだな。
English: Like a leaf...

Japanese: 自分は、その。 ‥‥頭脳派ッスから。
English: I'm just... You know... kinda on edge at the moment... If you know what I mean.

Japanese: カリヨーゼの従業員ッスね。
English: That's the girl who works over at Tender Lender.

Japanese: ‥‥ヘタに近よらないほうが いいッスよ。これはホントに。
English: You wanna stay away from her, OK? I mean it.

Japanese: たしかに、うらめしげ ですからねー、このひと‥‥。
English: She does look kind of unforgiving, doesn't she?

Japanese: ‥‥そういうコトじゃないッス。
English: That should be the least of your worries, pal.

Japanese: （どういうことだ‥‥？）
English: (What's that supposed to mean? What could be worse...?)

Japanese: 彼女の名前は、 鹿羽（しかばね）うらみ。
English: Her name's Viola Cadaverini.

Japanese: あの、鹿羽　権太（ごんた）の たったヒトリのマゴにあたるッス。
English: She's the only granddaughter of Bruto Cadaverini.

Japanese: ‥‥しかばね　ごんた‥‥？ 知ってる？　なるほどくん。
English: Bruto Cadaverini? Do you know who that is, Nick?

Japanese: 聞いたコトないよ。
English: Never heard of him.

Japanese: 鹿羽　権太は‥‥ 恐怖の《鹿羽組》組長ッス。
English: Bruto Cadaverini's the boss of the Cadaverini family.

Japanese: し、しかばねぐみ‥‥ （ゼッタイ、関わりたくないな）
English: The Cadaverinis? (That's one scary-sounding name!)

Japanese: 警察の力ですら、ヤツらを つぶすのはムズカシイッス。
English: We can't touch them. They're way too powerful for the police.

Japanese: それでも‥‥、戦うと言うッスか？
English: But you're thinking of taking them on, aren't you?

Japanese: だ、ダレもそんなコト 言ってませんよ！
English: N-No! I don't remember ever saying I was going to!

Japanese: （とにかく‥‥少しだけ、 話を聞いておくか‥‥）
English: (I better get some more info out of Gumshoe about this "family"...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 レストラン　吐麗美庵
English: January 7 Trés Bien

Japanese: あいかわらず いないねー、お客さん。
English: Empty, as usual.

Japanese: ヒルメシどきなのになあ。
English: Yeah, and it's lunchtime, too.

Japanese: よよ、よしッ！ きたきたきたきたきたアッ！
English: That's it! Come on, come on, come on!

Japanese: あれ。この声‥‥
English: Hey, that sounds like...

Japanese: そう！　そこで《８》ッス！ カモンッス！
English: Now just call an "8", pal! Come on! I know you can!

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、 ひとりでアツくなってるよ。
English: He's getting really worked up about something.

Japanese: うがああああああッ！ ハズしたッスかああッ！
English: Noooooo! That's the wrong number!

Japanese: 《８》なら５００円‥‥ ぐううう‥‥クヤシイッ！
English: Argh! Looks like an "8" would have only netted me five bucks anyway! What a rip off!

Japanese: なに、もだえてるんですか？ イトノコさん。
English: What's the problem, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: あ！　アンタたち‥‥
English: Huh!? Oh, it's you...

Japanese: ま、まあその。ははははは。 ちょっとその。ははははは。
English: I, uh... I was uh... Hah hah! I was just... Hah hah hah!

Japanese: ラジオ、聞いてたッス。
English: I was just listening to the radio, pal.
To the radio?

Japanese: ああっ！　イトノコさん、 ランチ食べてる！
English: Hey, Detective Gumshoe's having lunch here!

Japanese: （おお、ホントだ！ ニクハチセット‥‥）
English: (He is! And he's having the Twin-T set...)

Japanese: はああああ。 イイコトないッスねー‥‥
English: Aha ha ha... What can I say...?

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、アンタたち。 自分はそろそろ、署に戻るッス。
English: Well, I'm gonna head back to the precinct now.

Japanese: 捜査会議があるッスから。
English: We've got a big meeting starting in a bit.

Japanese: お。マコちゃんの事件ですか？
English: About Maggey's case, you mean?

Japanese: アレは、いちおう決着が ついたことになってるッス‥‥
English: No, that's pretty much wrapped up now...

Japanese: なんか今、大事件が 起きてるッスから、そっちッス。
English: There's another big case going down at the moment, so she's been pushed aside...

Japanese: わかりました。 じゃ、さよなら。イトノコさん。
English: OK. Well, see you later then.

Japanese: じゃッ！
English: Bye!

Japanese: は、早く帰ってくださいよ！
English: Y-You better get going, Detective, or you'll be late.

Japanese: ‥‥じ、ジツは‥‥
English: Actually, I, um...

Japanese: この糸鋸　圭介！ 一生のおねがいがあるッス。
English: I've kinda got a favor to ask. It's a big one!

Japanese: お、おねがい‥‥？
English: A favor?

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥ ま。マコクンに‥‥
English: Yeah... It's for, um... Maggey, actually.

Japanese: マコクンに‥‥、これを 差し入れしてほしいッス。
English: I was kinda hoping... you'd give this to her for me.

Japanese: こ、これは‥‥？
English: What is it?

Japanese: ベントウ‥‥ッス。 早起きして作ったッス。
English: It's a lunchbox. I got up early so I could make it.

Japanese: マコクン‥‥少し、 ヤセたみたいだったッスから。
English: I've been real worried about her. She looked like she'd lost a lot of weight.

Japanese: イトノコさん‥‥
English: Detective Gumshoe...

Japanese: イヤになるほどギッシリ つまってますね。ウインナー。
English: How many weenies are in here!? There's not a person on earth who could down this much meat.

Japanese: 自分には、イチバンの ゴチソウッスから。
English: You think? I love weenies. I can't get enough of their tender juiciness.

Japanese: おねがいッス！ いっしょうけんめい、作ったッス！
English: So will you give it to her? It took me ages to make, so please say you will, pal!

Japanese: （これは‥‥ 断れないよな、さすがに）
English: (I can't exactly say no, can I?)

Japanese: 《イトノコのベントウ》を 真宵ちゃんに持たせた。
English: Gumshoe's Lunchbox given to Maya to carry.

Japanese: 食べちゃうかもよ、あたし。
English: Maybe I'll eat it myself if I get hungry!

Japanese: じゃ、その‥‥ くれぐれもヨロシクッス！
English: Don't forget, OK? I'm counting on you to give that to Maggey.

Japanese: （やっと帰っていった‥‥）
English: (He's finally gone...)

Japanese: ファッション雑誌ばっかり。 しかも、フランス語だよ。
English: It's a rack full of fashion magazines. And they're all in French.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんも、たまには おしゃれしてみたら？
English: Why don't you try wearing something a bit more "chic" sometime huh, Maya?

Japanese: そうだなあ。たまには パリっぽくキメてみようかな‥‥
English: Yeah. I'd look stunning in some of those Parisian frocks, huh?

Japanese: 入り口のドアだ。
English: It's the restaurant's front entrance.

Japanese: パネルがぶら下がっていて、 フランス語で何やら書いてある。
English: There's a sign hanging on the door written in French.

Japanese: 《営業中》《準備中》の どちらかだろう。
English: It probably says "Open" or "Closed".

Japanese: どっちかわからないけど。 ‥‥フランス語だから。
English: It must be one or the other, but I don't know which since I dunno Jacques about French.

Japanese: 《入口》《出口》のどちらか かもしれないしね。
English: It could say "Entrance" or "Exit", too. Did you think of that?

Japanese: なんか、カワイイ小物が ちんまり並んでるよ。
English: Look at all the little trinkets tucked away in here.

Japanese: きっと、店長さんの シュミなんだろうね。
English: I bet Mr. Armstrong collected all of these personally.

Japanese: ブーケやら、ポプリやら、 ボーンチャイナのカップやら‥‥
English: Let's see... A bouquet of flowers, some potpourri, and look! Fine bone china cups!

Japanese: なるほどくん、ナニ人？
English: I never knew you were so... cultured.

Japanese: これぐらい、ニッポン人でも 知ってるよ！
English: Come on, Maya! This is common knowledge! Any Joe Schmoe knows this much!

Japanese: いくらでもお金が出てくる、 魔法のハコがあるよ。
English: Look, it's one of those magical boxes that spits out money.

Japanese: ‥‥レジをそう表現するヒトは 初めてだな。
English: You know, you're the only person who would ever describe a cash register in that way!

Japanese: ‥‥事件があった テーブル、だね。
English: This must be the table where the murder occurred.

Japanese: 立ち入り禁止の テープが張ってあるよ‥‥。
English: I guess so, with all this police tape all around it.

Japanese: テーブルのシミは‥‥ 毒入りのコーヒーかな。
English: And that stain must be from the poisoned coffee.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん。テーブルクロス、 ナメちゃダメだぞ。死ぬから。
English: Don't go licking the tablecloth. OK, Maya?

Japanese: ナメないよ！ あたしをナメないでよね！
English: Why would I lick it? I'm not a cat, you know!

Japanese: （お。ウマいコト言うな‥‥）
English: (Then why can I picture you doing just that?)

Japanese: キチンとセットされた テーブルだ。
English: This table's set nicely. It just needs a customer.

Japanese: これかな？　マメのオジさんが 言ってた《花ビン》って。
English: Hey, Nick. Do you think Old Seedy was talking about a vase like this today in court?

Japanese: たしかに、不安定、かつ とても割れやすそうな花ビンだな。
English: It does look like it's ready to fall over any second. Looks kind of fragile, too.

Japanese: お客さんが割るごとに １００万円ずつもらえば、
English: I bet if Mr. Armstrong charged 10,000 dollars for every vase a customer broke...

Japanese: 借金なんか、すぐ返せるのにね。
English: ...he'd be able to pay off his loan really quickly.

Japanese: ‥‥暴動が起こるぞ、 そんなコトしたら。
English: If you hit people with that kind of bill, Maya, they will hit you back!

Japanese: 窓のそとは、一面の銀世界だ。
English: Wow! It's a beautiful winter wonderland out there.

Japanese: え！　ウソ！ あたし、大好きなんだよ、雪。
English: Really!? Cool! I love snow! Let me see!

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。降ってないじゃない、 雪なんて。
English: Huh? It's not white. It's not even snowing.

Japanese: う、ウソに決まってるだろ。
English: Got ya! I was just kidding, Maya.

Japanese: なるほどくんッ！
English: Nick!

Japanese: 言っていいウソと 悪いウソがあるよッ！
English: There are lies that are OK to tell, and lies that definitely aren't.

Japanese: （悪いウソだったのか‥‥今の）
English: (...All I did was tell an itty-bitty white lie about nonexistent white snow.)

Japanese: テーブルの間に、 仕切りが立っている。
English: This restaurant has partitions that separate the tables.

Japanese: 席について見えるのは、 向かい側のテーブルだけ、か。
English: When you're seated at a table, you can only see the tables to your right or left.

Japanese: ホントだ。おとなりの席、 ゼンゼン見えないよ。
English: Wow, you really can't see the table in front or behind you at all!

Japanese: 相手のコーヒーに毒を入れるには もってこいだね。
English: It's the perfect setup for slipping poison into your enemy's coffee.

Japanese: もう、サイアクッス！
English: This is a nightmare!

Japanese: 自分は‥‥マコクンに 合わせるカオがないッス！
English: How am I supposed to look Maggey in the eye now, pal!?

Japanese: いい調子で追いつめてました からねー、マコちゃんのこと。
English: You really drove her into a corner, you know.

Japanese: どーして、今日にかぎって やっつけてくれなかったッスか！
English: You always blow apart my testimony! Why, of all days, didn't you do it today!?

Japanese: す、スミマセン。めずらしく スキがなかったから‥‥
English: S-Sorry... There just weren't any holes in it... for once!

Japanese: いつもは、スキだらけで スカスカなのにね。
English: Yeah, what happened? Usually, your testimonies are like Swiss cheese!

Japanese: す‥‥スカスカッスか。
English: S-Swiss cheese...?

Japanese: （まあ‥‥当たり前の ハナシなんだよな）
English: (Would he have preferred, "crumbly like aged parmesan"?)

Japanese: （一度は判決が下ってる事件だ。 そう、スキがあるワケがない）
English: (Anyway, this case has already been ruled on. There shouldn't be any holes left to find.)

Japanese: あの‥‥マコクン、ナニか 言ってたッスか？　自分のコト。
English: So... Did Maggey say anything to you? About me, I mean.

Japanese: そうですねー‥‥
English: Well, um... How did she put it again...?

Japanese: イトノコセンパイ、ヒドいッス！
English: I can't believe Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: もー、キライッス！ カオも見たくないッス！
English: I hate him, sir! I mean it! I don't ever want to see him again!

Japanese: ‥‥とか、なんとか。
English: Something like that.

Japanese: う‥‥‥‥ うおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: Wah... Waaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: あ！　イトノコさん！
English: P-Please! Detective Gumshoe! I didn't mean...

Japanese: 自分は！　自分はああああああッ！
English: Why!? Why is this happening!?

Japanese: カベにアタマぶつけてる‥‥
English: He's banging his head against the wall, Nick!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥カワイソウに）
English: (Oh, man... Poor Gumshoe...)

Japanese: イトノコ刑事。 どうですか？　ニクハチセットは。
English: So, did you like the Twin-T set?

Japanese: こんな高いヒルメシは 生まれてはじめてッス。
English: I've never paid that much money for lunch before.

Japanese: 食べてて、あまりのキンチョーに 手がふるえるッス。
English: I was so nervous, my hands were shaking.

Japanese: で？　で？　おアジのほうは‥‥？
English: So? How did it taste!?

Japanese: そッスねー。
English: Well...

Japanese: さすが２９８０円だけあって、 なんというか‥‥珍味ッス。
English: For 20 bucks, I guess... I dunno how to describe it, really. It was... delicate.

Japanese: ちんみ‥‥
English: Delicate?

Japanese: あの。刑事さん。 マズくないんですか？　それ。
English: You mean you liked it!? It didn't taste bad to you?

Japanese: ど、どうしたのかな、 イトノコさん。
English: What's the matter with him?

Japanese: なにやら、ジッと考えこんでるぞ。
English: Looks like he's thinking...

Japanese: それッスゥッ！
English: That's it!

Japanese: じつはさっきから、この料理に ふさわしいコトバを探してたッス。
English: I've been trying to think of the right word to describe the taste.

Japanese: 今、わかったッス！ “マズい”だったッス！
English: I just got it right now... "Bad"! That's it. It tasted bad!

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: *sigh*

Japanese: ２９８０円も払ったから、その ジジツを認められなかったッス！
English: It's kinda hard to admit it to yourself when you pay 20 bucks though, pal.

Japanese: （‥‥食べ終わってから 教えてあげればよかったな）
English: (Maybe he should've found out about the price AFTER he had finished eating...)

Japanese: 被害者の岡　高夫さんも、 わざわざ食べに来たのかな。
English: Maybe that's why Glen Elg came here.

Japanese: 恐るべきニクハチセットの ウワサを聞きつけて。
English: Maybe he heard about the famous Twin-T set!

Japanese: （“被害者”、か。 そういえば、今さらだけど‥‥）
English: (Glen Elg, the victim, huh... Which reminds me.)

Japanese: （岡　高夫の情報、ほとんど ないんだよな‥‥）
English: (We still hardly know anything about the guy.)

Japanese: せっかくだし、聞いてみる？ イトノコさんに。
English: Why don't we ask Gumshoe what he knows, seeing as he's here?

Japanese: そういえば‥‥ひとりで 大サワギしてましたよね。
English: So what were you all excited about earlier?

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: あ！　そうそう。 “ラジオを聞いてた”とか。
English: That's right! You said you were listening to the radio or something.

Japanese: べ、ベツにそんな！ 大サワギなんて‥‥
English: Oh, that? That was nothing! I-I wasn't excited!

Japanese: ね。ね。ナニ聞いて コーフンしてたんですか？
English: Come on, Detective Gumshoe! You can tell little ol' me! What were you listening to?

Japanese: いや、その‥‥ ラ、《ラジオ体操》ッス。
English: N-Nothing, really. It was just the, um... "Daily Exercise Show"!

Japanese: （な、なんだ！ サイコ・ロック‥‥？）
English: (What the...!? A Psyche-Lock?)

Japanese: ははは。オイシイッスねー、 オマールえびは！
English: Hah hah hah! Mmm... This lunch special's lobster is great!

Japanese: イトノコさん、 声がウラがえってるよ‥‥
English: Then why are there tears in your eyes...?

Japanese: ねえねえ、 教えてくださいよー、ラジオ。
English: Come on, Detective Gumshoe. You can tell us what you were listening to on the radio.

Japanese: いや、だからその。 の、《のどジマン》ッス。
English: But I already told you. It was the, um... the... weather forecast, pal.

Japanese: （さっきは《ラジオ体操》って 言ってたじゃないか！）
English: (Didn't you say it was an exercise program earlier!?)

Japanese: いやー、それにしても あれッスねー。はははは。
English: It was nothing, really. Hah hah hah!

Japanese: （大したコトじゃないけど‥‥ かくされると、気になるよな）
English: (Even if it's nothing, the fact that he's hiding it makes me really curious!)

Japanese: けっこう当たるもんだな、 と思って、買ってみたッス。
English: I'm usually pretty lucky so I figured I'd give it a try.

Japanese: 宝クジ、ですか。
English: What's with everyone and the lottery...?

Japanese: で？　で？　どうでした？
English: So? How did it go?

Japanese: ５０円、当たったッス。
English: I won 50 cents!

Japanese: ゼンゼン当たらないより、 かえってハラが立ったッス！
English: It'd be better to win nothing at all than half a lousy buck! I was so mad!

Japanese: （キモチはわかるな‥‥）
English: (Yeah, I know the feeling.)

Japanese: 岡　高夫と同じクジを 買ったッスがねえ‥‥。
English: I bought the same kind of ticket as Mr. Elg.

Japanese: ラジオ番組と連動していて、 なかなかスリリングだったッス。
English: When they read those results out on the radio, it really gets your heart pumping!

Japanese: クジの抽選のようすを、ナマ放送。 これが燃えるッス。
English: You know they broadcast it live when they're picking the numbers? It's intense, pal!

Japanese: 岡　高夫さんも、それを聞いて いたんですねー、きっと。
English: I bet that's what Glen was listening to on the day he was killed.

Japanese: ところで‥‥ 今、何時ですか？
English: Yeah. What time is it now?

Japanese: ‥‥え。 １時半、ちょっとすぎ‥‥ッス。
English: Uh, it's... It's just after 1:30.

Japanese: その、宝クジのラジオ番組は、 いつもこの時間に‥‥？
English: And are the lottery results always broadcast at the same time?

Japanese: そッス。‥‥クジ売り場で もらったチラシがあるッスよ。
English: Yeah. Look, I got this flyer when I bought the ticket.

Japanese: 《長者ラジオのチラシ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Millionaire Radio Flyer added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 《長者ラジオ》‥‥かあ。 いいね、“長者”ってヒビキ。
English: "Millionaire Radio"? That sounds cool!

Japanese: なるほどくん！ あたしもやってみたい！
English: I want to try it, Nick!

Japanese: 自分のおこづかいで買えよ。
English: Then buy a ticket, Maya. With your own money!

Japanese: ～ラジオ～
English: -- The Radio --

Japanese: さあ、イトノコ刑事。 ‥‥聞かせてもらいましょうか。
English: Alright, Detective Gumshoe, tell me the truth. What were you listening to?

Japanese: ううう。あらたまって聞かれると、 よけい言いにくいッス‥‥。
English: No way, pal. Now that you've made a big thing out of it, I'm not gonna tell you...

Japanese: すぐ、ラクにしてあげますよ。
English: We'll see about that, pal.

Japanese: あなたが、ラジオを聴きながら 絶叫していた内容を考えれば‥‥
English: Considering all the noise you were making while you were listening...

Japanese: だいたいわかりますからね。 聞いていた番組の内容は‥‥！
English: ...it's pretty clear what kind of radio program it was. I'd say it was related to...

Japanese: いかがですか！　イトノコ刑事！
English: Well, Detective Gumshoe!?

Japanese: そのとおりッス！　さすがッス！ 大セイカイッス！　１等賞ッス！
English: Yeah! That's it, pal! You got me. You sure are something!

Japanese: （‥‥と、言いつつ、 ロックはそのまんま、か）
English: (Then how come I'm still seeing the lock?)

Japanese: （どうやら、シッパイした らしいな‥‥）
English: (Guess I was wrong...)

Japanese: （ラジオを聞いていたときの イトノコ刑事は‥‥）
English: (Now, what was it he shouting when he was listening to the radio...?)

Japanese: よよ、よしッ！ きたきたきたきたきたアッ！
English: That's it! Come on, come on, come on!

Japanese: そう！　そこで《８》ッス！ カモンッス！
English: Now just call an "8", pal! Come on! I know you can!

Japanese: うがああああああッ！ ハズしたッスかああッ！
English: Noooooo! That's the wrong number!

Japanese: （わかりきったコトだ。 もう一度、やってみよう‥‥）
English: (It seems so obvious... I'd better ask him again.)

Japanese: ま、まだ あきらめないッスか‥‥
English: D-Don't tell me you're not through yet...

Japanese: もしかして、イトノコ刑事‥‥
English: I'm right, aren't I?

Japanese: 宝くじの当選番号の発表を 聞いていたんじゃないですか？
English: You were listening to the lottery results, weren't you?

Japanese: 被害者の岡　高夫にあやかって、 デカいのを当てようとした！
English: You thought you'd try to win big, just like Glen Elg did!
I-It's...

Japanese: アンタは超能力者ッスゥゥッ！
English: It's like you can see right through me!

Japanese: （な、なんだ‥‥？ もうオシマイかよ！）
English: (Huh? I've cracked him already...?)

Japanese: ‥‥だから、かえって 言いだせなかったッスゥゥゥゥ！
English: That's why I said it was nothing!

Japanese: アンタと戦うために、 この再審理を担当しているッス。
English: Mr. Godot agreed to take this case just so he could face you, pal.

Japanese: 他の、もっと大きな事件も そのためにキャンセルして‥‥
English: He was working on a bunch of more important cases, but he cancelled them all...

Japanese: あれ。でも、それなら‥‥
English: Huh? But then...

Japanese: １ヶ月前の、マコちゃんの 最初の法廷のときは‥‥
English: ...why didn't Mr. Godot take this case...

Japanese: どうしてゴドー検事、 出て来なかったんだろ。
English: ...when Maggey's first trial came up?

Japanese: そういえば、そうだな。
English: Yeah, that is kinda odd. I mean, everyone thought the defense was "Phoenix Wright".

Japanese: ニセモノとはいえ、担当弁護士は 《成歩堂　龍一》だったのに。
English: So why didn't he try to fight "me" then?

Japanese: ゴドー検事によれば‥‥
English: Well, according to Mr. Godot...

Japanese: 『ひとめ見て、ニセモノとわかった から、ほっといた』そうッス。
English: ...he knew the guy was a phony right away, so he didn't want to bother with the trial.

Japanese: （ほっとかずに、 裁判長に言ってくれよ！）
English: (He couldn't be bothered to tell the judge!?)

Japanese: ムカシ‥‥一度だけ、 尋問を受けたことがあるッス。
English: She cross-examined me once, you know.

Japanese: え‥‥千尋さんに、ですか？
English: What? Mia did?

Japanese: あれは‥‥名勝負だったッス。
English: Yeah. That was us. The big guns locking horns.

Japanese: 証人対弁護士！　アツいアツい カケヒキが展開されたッス。
English: Witness versus lawyer! It was a battle of cunning tactics and tricks.

Japanese: （証人がカケヒキしちゃ マズいだろ‥‥）
English: (But the witness isn't supposed to play any tricks...)

Japanese: おしいヒトを亡くしたッス‥‥。
English: I can't believe she's gone...

Japanese: あいかわらず、元気そうッスね。
English: You're looking upbeat, as usual.

Japanese: イトノコさんも、あいかわらず つかれたカオしてるッスね！
English: And you're looking tired as usual, Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: 同じニンゲンなのに、 この差はいったい、なんなんス‥‥
English: Yeah. That's life, huh? One guy's got it all, and one guy's got squat.

Japanese: あれ。ボヤき始めたよ。
English: Is it just me or did the room get darker all of a sudden...?

Japanese: ま。ま。きっと、今年は イトノコさんの年になりますよ！
English: You know, Detective. This is the year of the, um... gum!

Japanese: そ‥‥そッスかねえ。
English: I-It is?

Japanese: ええ。たぶん。
English: Yeah. ...I think.

Japanese: そ‥‥そッスよね！
English: Gum, huh? This is gonna be a great year for this ol' Gumshoe then, right?

Japanese: あは。元気になったよ。
English: Look at that. He perked up in no time!

Japanese: 遊ぶなよ‥‥
English: Maya... Don't mess with him like that.

Japanese: 初めて見たッス。あそこまで しどろもどろな証人。
English: You know, in all my years, I've never seen a witness who was that confused before.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事が カスんで見えましたよ。
English: Yeah, you looked pretty confused by his testimony.

Japanese: 自分は、ああいうジイさんは キライじゃないッス。
English: Nothing against old guys, I like them and all...
But...

Japanese: ジイさんは、見たコトを正直に 話しているように見えたッス。
English: He sounded like he was pretty sure about what he saw.

Japanese: そうですよね！ あたしも、そう思いました！
English: Yeah, that's the impression I got, too!

Japanese: （じつは、ぼくも そんな気がしている）
English: (Me, three...)

Japanese: （でも‥‥それならなぜ、あんなに ジジツと食いちがうんだ‥‥？）
English: (But then, why doesn't even a single part of his testimony corroborate any of the facts?)

Japanese: 見るからに“オニ刑事”って 感じッスよねー。
English: I know I look a little rough around the edges.

Japanese: じつは最近、ますます 刑事っぽさがアップしてきたッス。
English: But the truth is, I'm a lot more "detective-like" than I used to be.

Japanese: へええ。どのへんがですか？
English: Really? Like what part?

Japanese: ‥‥コートの色あいとか。
English: ...Like the color of my coat.

Japanese: あ！　言われてみれば。
English: Ah! You're right!

Japanese: （コートの中身のほうは どうなんだろう‥‥）
English: (Yeah, but what about what's inside the coat...?)

Japanese: マコくん、今もヒトリ、 独房でむせび泣いてるッスかね。
English: Maggey's probably crying all alone right now, huh...

Japanese: ええ。イトノコさんのせいで。
English: Yeah, thanks to you.

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！ ゴメンッス！　スマンッス！
English: Aaaaaaargh! I'm sorry! I'm so sorry!

Japanese: やめとけよ。ココロのキズに ワサビをすりこむの。
English: Maya! Don't torch poor Gumshoe feelings like they are some sort of cheap crème brûlée!

Japanese: マコちゃんに《ホの字》 なんだねー、イトノコさん。
English: You're really crazy about Maggey, aren't you?

Japanese: いや！　ベツに、自分はアレッス！ そういうナニじゃなくて、その！
English: No! No, no! I'm just... I'm not... It's not that I...

Japanese: （ムリしなくていいのに）
English: (You don't have to hide it, Gumshoe! It's pretty obvious.)

Japanese: ううん。ブサイクなんだねー、 イトノコさん。
English: Yeah, you're way too plain-looking for Maggey. Right?

Japanese: それを言うなら《ブキヨウ》ッス！
English: I'd arrest you right now if you weren't so right...

Japanese: かなりウデのいい プログラマーだったッス。
English: This guy was a real programming genius.

Japanese: 職場じゃ《歩くコンピュータ》 と呼ばれていたらしいッス。
English: They called him "the walking computer" at the place where he worked.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、そもそも プログラムを作る必要、ないね。
English: What happens when he crashes though!? Does he just stop moving all of a sudden!?

Japanese: （そういうコトじゃない）
English: (*groan* He wasn't a computer, Maya!)

Japanese: とにかく、マコクンと 接点がないのはたしかッス。
English: Anyway, there's nothing between Maggey and the victim.

Japanese: ‥‥きのう聞いたハナシと、 あまり変わりませんね。
English: Yeah. That's what we found out yesterday, too.

Japanese: ね。ね。もっと楽しい情報は ないんですか？
English: Hey, Detective Gumshoe. Don't you have any information that's a bit more fun?

Japanese: た、楽しい情報、ッスか？ そッスねー‥‥‥‥‥‥
English: F-Fun? I, uh...

Japanese: あ、そッス！　いっそ、 彼の職場に行ってみるッスか？
English: Oh! I know! So, have you paid a visit to where Elg worked yet? You might as well.

Japanese: 岡　高夫さんの‥‥？
English: His workplace? Where's that?

Japanese: たしか‥‥《バグダス》とかいう コンピュータ会社ッス。
English: A computer firm called "Blue Screens, Inc." or something like that.

Japanese: なんか、ヤリ手っぽい名前ですね！ おもしろそうだよ、なるほどくん！
English: Sounds like a real stable company. This could be fun, Nick. Let's go!

Japanese: ‥‥キカイ、 ニガテなんだよな、ぼく。
English: Computers aren't really my thing, Maya.

Japanese: だいじょうぶ！　“はいてく”なら 真宵ちゃんにまかせて！
English: We'll be fine! I know all about that hi-tech stuff.

Japanese: （ホントかよ‥‥）
English: (I wonder about that...)

Japanese: このレストランの近くッスから。 行ってみるといいッス！
English: It's just around the corner from this joint. You should take a look.

Japanese: （コンピュータ会社 《バグダス》‥‥か‥‥）
English: (A computer firm called "Blue Screens, Inc." huh...)

Japanese: バグダスの社長さんッスな。
English: That lady is the boss of Blue Screens, Inc., pal.

Japanese: そッスね‥‥。 特にアヤシイ点はないッス。
English: Yeah... I figure she's clean. She got nothing to hide.

Japanese: なんというか、お人形みたいな ヒトだったッス。
English: She seemed kinda like a doll to me.

Japanese: いいイミでも、悪いイミでも。
English: In a good way and a bad way.

Japanese: ‥‥そこが、 ちょっとアヤシイッスかねー。
English: Kinda makes you think she might be hiding something.

Japanese: 会うなり『アナタ、カラダが毒素で パンパンね』なんて言われて‥‥
English: You know what that chef said to me? "Oh là là! Your body is full of la toxins."

Japanese: このビンをわたされたッス。
English: And then he gave me this bottle.

Japanese: ラベルには“ジュニパー”って 書いてありますね。
English: What's in it?

Japanese: ゆうべ、おフロにちょっと しのばせてみたッス。
English: I don't know. The label says, "Juniper".

Japanese: よく眠れたッス。
English: But I'll tell you what. This stuff works like a charm. I slept like a rock last night.

Japanese: はあ‥‥よかったですね。
English: O-Oh, really... That's nice...

Japanese: あの。これなんですけど‥‥
English: What do you make of this...?

Japanese: 今は‥‥マコクンのコトで アタマがいっぱいッス。
English: Sorry, pal. All I can think about is Maggey at the moment.
‥‥‥‥‥‥<Phoenix>

Japanese: ちち、ちがうッス！　そういう イミじゃなくて、自分は‥‥！
English: N-No! I didn't mean it like that! I meant...

Japanese: （ムリしなくていいのに‥‥）
English: (You don't have to explain... Really...)

Japanese: ホンモノッスか？　それ。
English: Is that thing real, pal?

Japanese: ‥‥見せると みんなにそう言われます。
English: Why does everyone keep asking me that?

Japanese: どんなヤツッスかねー、 アンタのニセモノ‥‥
English: I wonder what this phony of yours is like...

Japanese: マコちゃんを、ワザと 有罪にしたヤツですよ。
English: He had Maggey found guilty of murder. Doesn't that tell you?

Japanese: たたきツブすッス！
English: It tells me the guy is cruising for a bruising!

Japanese: アツいねー、イトノコさん。 いろんなイミで。
English: You're really caring, aren't you, Detective Gumshoe? About Maggey, I mean.

Japanese: 自分も、その裁判は 傍聴していないッスが‥‥
English: I wasn't at the trial myself.

Japanese: 知り合いの刑事に聞いてみたッス。 このときの、アンタの弁護のコト。
English: But I asked this one detective I know how your defense was.

Japanese: ど、どんな感じでしたか？
English: And what did he say?

Japanese: 『あきれてモノも言えないよ』 ‥‥と、前置きしてから‥‥
English: He started off by saying, "I'm at a complete loss for words..."

Japanese: １時間ぐらい聞かされたッス。 アンタの無能ぶりを。
English: But he must have found some quick because he went on about how bad you were for an hour.

Japanese: うう‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: どう見ても、ワザと 有罪にしたんじゃないか、って。
English: He said you sucked so much, it seemed like you were trying to get Maggey found guilty!

Japanese: （ワザと‥‥か‥‥）
English: (It looked like I was "trying to get Maggey found guilty"...?)

Japanese: 被害者がラクガキした スポーツ新聞ッスな。
English: That's the sports paper the victim wrote something in, right?

Japanese: どうかしましたか？
English: What is it?

Japanese: やっぱり、どこかで 聞いたことがあるッス。
English: I know I've heard that name somewhere before...

Japanese: “くりーにんぐ・ぼんばー” しかも、ごく最近。
English: "MC Bomber"... Yeah... I heard it real recently, too...

Japanese: （‥‥いつになく、マジメに 考えこんでいる‥‥）
English: (Wow, he actually seems to be thinking for once!)

Japanese: ダメ。思い出せないッス。
English: Ack, it's no good. I can't remember...

Japanese: （‥‥いつもどおり、ダメだった）
English: (...And he goes back to being the Gumshoe we all know and love.)

Japanese: ‥‥それについて、 多く語ることはないッス。
English: I haven't got anything to say about that, pal.

Japanese: 早く、引っこめてもらいたいッス。
English: Just take it away already, please?

Japanese: （よっぽどイタかったみたいだな。 ２９８０円は‥‥）
English: (Spending 20 dollars on this has got to be killing him inside...)

Japanese: ５０００万円ッスよ！
English: It's for half a million dollars, pal!

Japanese: １円玉で、えーと‥‥ ざっと５０００万枚ッス！
English: That's... um... half a million dollar bills!

Japanese: （今の“えーと”は なんだったんだ‥‥？）
English: (What was the "um" in there for!?)

Japanese: 今回の事件、そんな 大金がカラんでいるんですか？
English: Is there really that much money tied up in this case?

Japanese: 資料を見てないから 知らないッス。
English: I can't give you an answer on that, pal. Not without this case's file in front of me.

Japanese: でも‥‥
English: But I'll tell you this...

Japanese: 本土坊が、金のためなら なんでもやる状況にいたコトは、
English: That Armstrong guy would've done anything for cash.

Japanese: やぶさかでないッス。
English: He was desperate, you know?

Japanese: ‥‥よくわからないけど、 なんとなくわかりました。
English: No, I don't. But I think I sort of get the picture.

Japanese: シアワセ行きの 片道きっぷッスね。
English: A one-way ticket to happiness, huh?

Japanese: 岡　高夫さん、コレの せいで死んじゃったんですよ。
English: Glen Elg died because of this ticket.

Japanese: それでも、シアワセ行きの 片道きっぷッス。
English: Yeah, but it's still a one-way ticket to happiness, pal.

Japanese: マコちゃん、コレのせいで 殺人の疑いをかけられたんですよ。
English: And Maggey was found guilty of murder because of it, too.

Japanese: ‥‥マコちゃんの話題は やっぱり、マズいみたいだね。
English: ...Looks like anything about Maggey is an instant conversation stopper.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事のココロの、イチバン やわらかい部分を直撃するらしい。
English: Yeah. He's got a real soft spot for her, and it obviously hurts when you hit it!

Japanese: ケッキョク、その袋の 中身は見つかっていないッス。
English: We never did find the contents of that bag.

Japanese: 破れたコマクの おクスリだったんですよね。
English: It was medicine for Mr. Elg's ruptured eardrum, right?

Japanese: 岡　高夫の左耳から、クスリが 検出されているッス。
English: Yeah. We found traces of it in his left ear canal.

Japanese: 吐麗美庵で使ったのは、 まず、まちがいないッス‥‥
English: He must've used it while he was at Trés Bien. We're sure of that much.

Japanese: マコクンがつけていた エプロンッスね。
English: That's the apron Maggey was wearing.

Japanese: ‥‥マコクンのカオリが するッス。
English: Yeah, it still smells like her, too.

Japanese: マコちゃんって、ケチャップの ニオイがするのかな。
English: Does this mean Maggey smells like ketchup?

Japanese: 人気があるみたいッスよ。 このラジオ番組。
English: Apparently everyone's listening to this show now.

Japanese: まあ、やっぱり ほしいですからねえ。おカネ。
English: That's because everyone wants money.

Japanese: 被害者の岡　高夫は、 ギャンブルが大好きだったッス。
English: They say that the victim, Glen Elg, was really into gambling.

Japanese: あたしも大好きッスよ、 ギャンブル。
English: Yep. You can't beat gambling! I love it, too!

Japanese: ゆうべも、なるほどくんに トランプで５万円、勝ったし。
English: I won 500 dollars last night playing cards with Nick.

Japanese: え！　あれ、ホンキだったの？
English: Huh? We were playing for money!?

Japanese: トーゼンでしょ！ ちゃんと払ってよね！
English: Of course! So you'd better pay up!

Japanese: 刑事の前で、ダイタンな 発言をするッスね‥‥
English: You're a smart one! ...Waiting for a cop to be present before asking for the cash!

Japanese: お。売ってくれるッスか？ このスクーター。
English: Hey! Are you putting this up for sale, pal?

Japanese: ち、ちがいますよ！
English: N-No!

Japanese: ‥‥って、ほしいんですか？ こんなポンコツ。
English: Why, would you want a busted up scooter like this anyway?

Japanese: サドルなんて、ハトのフン まみれですよ。
English: Yeah. Plus, the seat's all covered in pigeon poop!

Japanese: ナニにまみれていようと、 走ればモンダイないッス！
English: Who cares? If it runs, that's all that matters to me.

Japanese: （ぼくのニセモノが乗っていた スクーター‥‥）
English: (My phony was riding this bike...)

Japanese: （あの公園に行けば、また あるかもしれないな‥‥）
English: (Maybe if I head back to the park, it'll be there again.)

Japanese: どうですか？ これなんですけど‥‥
English: What do you think about this, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: すまねッスが‥‥
English: Sorry, pal...

Japanese: 事件に関する証拠については、 しゃべれないッス。
English: I can't discuss anything connected to the case.

Japanese: えー。カタいこと 言わないヤクソクじゃないですか！
English: Hey! I thought you said you were going to help us out!

Japanese: ‥‥たいした情報がない、 というウラ事情もあるッス。
English: I can't help you out if I don't have any info!

Japanese: まあ、裁判で審理されたもの ばっかりですからね‥‥
English: Yeah, I suppose the retrial has only just begun...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 レストラン　吐麗美庵
English: January 7 Trés Bien

Japanese: あ‥‥あら。あなたたち。 お待ちしてたわぁ。
English: Eh, bonjour! I 'ave been waiting for you to return.

Japanese: ‥‥本土坊さん‥‥
English: Mr. Armstrong...

Japanese: ちょうどよかった。ぼくたちも お話を聞きたいと‥‥
English: Ah, good timing. I was hoping to find you here. We'd like to ask you a few questions...

Japanese: こっちには、 話すコトなんかあらへんねや！
English: Well, he hasn't got anythin' to say to youse fellas!

Japanese: あ！　ニセなるほどくん‥‥
English: Ah! It's Xin Eohp...

Japanese: だァれが なるほどくんじゃいぃぃィィィッ！
English: Who you callin' "Zinnee Ooooope"!?

Japanese: きゃわははわあああああっ！
English: Aaaaah!

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん、テーブルの下に かくれちまったぞ‥‥！）
English: (Come out from under the table already, Maya!)

Japanese: ‥‥さ。 わたしてもらおうかい。
English: OK. Hand it over!

Japanese: な、ナニをですか‥‥？
English: Wh-What?

Japanese: トボけんなや！ 診断書よ、診断書ォ‥‥
English: Youse wanna play games wid me? I don't recommend dat! The medical papers! Now!

Japanese: （うらみさんのアレ、か‥‥）
English: (Uh, oh... I think he wants Viola Cadaverini's papers back...)

Japanese: ‥‥これ、ですか。 “１億円”の診断書‥‥
English: Y-You mean... this? The million dollar medical papers?

Japanese: ‥‥うらみさんは、あなたを 信じていた。
English: Ms. Cadaverini trusted you.

Japanese: だから‥‥“あなたを手伝った” そう言ってましたよ。
English: That's why she said that she helped you.

Japanese: ‥‥クソがッ！　あのバカ女ァ‥‥
English: Forget about it! That girl's dumber dan an eggplant.

Japanese: しめっぽいのは ツラだけにしろっちゅうねん！
English: Youse wanna know what's sad? I'll tell youse what's sad. And it ain't only her face.

Japanese: ホンマ、組長のマゴや思うて デカいカオしおって‥‥
English: She thinks she's got power cuz she's Bruto's little girl... Now THAT's sad...

Japanese: この診断書はわたせない。
English: I can't let you have these papers.

Japanese: この“１億円”を用意するために、 あなたがナニをやったか‥‥
English: Tomorrow in court...

Japanese: 明日の法廷で、立証する！
English: ...I'm going to expose what you did to get the one million you used to pay this off!

Japanese: アホな弁護士さんやのォ。
English: Are you crazy or somethin'!?

Japanese: アンタにわたす気がなくても、 こっちにはカンケイないんや。
English: I don't care if you wanna give it to me or not!

Japanese: なにしろ‥‥こっちは、 ２人がかりや。‥‥せやな？
English: There's two of us here. You got dat? Two.

Japanese: ウイ。‥‥そうね。
English: Err... oui! Oui, oui!

Japanese: ほ‥‥本土坊さん‥‥！
English: Mr. Armstrong!?

Japanese: ユルして‥‥。ワタシ‥‥ 逆らえないのよ‥‥。
English: Forgive me! Désolé! I cannot argue wiz 'im!

Japanese: う、うぐ‥‥ッ！ （な‥‥殴られた‥‥！）
English: U-Urgh! (...That... really hurt!)

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ‥‥ いただくぜ‥‥コイツは。
English: Is dat all youse got? I'll be takin' dose papers now.

Japanese: 本土坊！　‥‥ライター、 持ってこいやッ！
English: Armstrong! Get that lighter!

Japanese: ま‥‥待て‥‥ッ！
English: W-Wait...!

Japanese: ザンネンやったな‥‥成歩堂。
English: Don't take it too hard... ...Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: （最後のサイゴで‥‥ 油断、したか‥‥）
English: (That was so stupid... I shouldn't... have let my guard down.)

Japanese: （あの診断書は‥‥ 決定的な‥‥証‥‥拠‥‥‥）
English: (Those medical papers were... vital... evi...dence...)

Japanese: 待つッスぅぅぅぅッ！
English: Hold it, pal!

Japanese: い‥‥イトノコ刑事‥‥ッ！
English: D-Detective Gumshoe!?

Japanese: け‥‥刑事ィィ‥‥？
English: D-Detective!?

Japanese: ダレだろうとカンケイあらへんでェ コラアアアアアアァァァァッ！
English: Youse think you gonna stop me, copper!? Beat it! Gwaaaaaaaaaaar!

Japanese: ここここ、恐ェッス‥‥！ イヤ、ガマンッス！
English: Whoa! C-Come on, Gumshoe! Keep it together!

Japanese: アンタ！　こ、この場は 自分にまかせて、逃げるッス！
English: You guys! Get outta here! Leave this guy to me.
B-But...

Japanese: いいからッ！ これ、持っていくッス！
English: Go, pal! And take this!

Japanese: アンタがいなくて‥‥ ダレがマコクンを助けるッスかッ！
English: If you get hurt, who'd look after Maggey, huh!?

Japanese: わ‥‥わかりました！ おねがいしますッ！
English: A-Alright! Thanks, Gumshoe!

Japanese: あっ、なるほどくん！ 置いてかないでよー！
English: Wait, Nick! Don't leave me behind!

Japanese: （明日の法廷で‥‥きっと！ この借りは、返す‥‥！）
English: (I'll get even with that guy tomorrow... in court!)

Japanese: （カリヨーゼには‥‥ずいぶん、 いろいろ借りてしまったからな）
English: (Tender Lender is going down!)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 吐麗美庵　キッチン
English: January 7 Trés Bien Kitchen

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。
English: Huh?

Japanese: 店長さん、だれかと話してるよ。
English: Mr. Armstrong's talking to someone.

Japanese: ‥‥来月、また来ますから‥‥
English: I'll be back next month.

Japanese: おま、おま、おま、 お待ちしてるわあ！
English: Oui! Naturellement! I will be waiting for you!

Japanese: ‥‥今度いただけなかったら‥‥ つけますね‥‥火‥‥
English: If you haven't got it by then, I'm afraid it might get a little... hot... around here.

Japanese: い、いやだワ！ ちゃんと用意するから！
English: Non! I will 'ave everything ready, I promise!

Japanese: ‥‥スキなんです、火を見るの‥‥ ククッ‥‥
English: I love fire, you know... I love the way it crackles... Hee... Hee... Hee...

Japanese: ノノノ、ノンノンノン！ やめてちょうだい！
English: Non, non, non! Stop it! I beg you!

Japanese: ‥‥ウラ切らないでくださいね‥‥ わたし‥‥いつも、見てます‥‥
English: Then don't let me down... I'll be watching you...

Japanese: そ、そんな！ し、し、信じてちょうだいよお。
English: Mais, non! Z'is is not necessary! You can trust me, mademoiselle!

Japanese: ‥‥しゃべったら‥‥刺しますから ‥‥包丁で‥‥
English: Talk to anyone... and I'll drive a knife... right through your heart.

Japanese: だだ、だいじょうぶよ。 もお、心配屋さんなんだから！
English: Oh, non! You do not 'ave to worry. You know, you worry far too much.

Japanese: そんなあなたには、これよお。 サンダルウッドの香水‥‥
English: Maybe z'is will 'elp you relax. It is la oil of sandalwood.

Japanese: ‥‥スキなんです‥‥おさしみ‥‥ ククッ‥‥。
English: I do love... raw meat... from time to time... Hee... Hee... Hee...

Japanese: きゃああああああっ！
English: Aaaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、わたし‥‥ 帰りますから‥‥
English: I'll be taking my leave... Goodbye... for now!

Japanese: ‥‥うううう‥‥。 キモチワルい！
English: Urgh! I 'ave la shivers!

Japanese: シオでも まいておこうかしらね！
English: I must rub some of my oil all over my body before I become z'e nervous wreck!

Japanese: そおれ。オーエス、オーエス！
English: Z'ere. Oh, oui, oui! Z'at feels good!

Japanese: うぎゃわっ！
English: Urgh!

Japanese: あ、あらあらあら！ ゴメンナサイ。
English: Oh là là! Excusez-moi, monsieur!

Japanese: めめ、目が！　目があああっ！
English: M-My eyes! My EYES!

Japanese: そんなときは、これ。 サンダルウッドの香水よお。
English: Your eyes? If you 'ave trouble wi'z your eyes, you need z'is. La oil of sandalwood!

Japanese: さっきの あまりものじゃないですか！
English: Isn't this just the leftovers of what you were just using!?

Japanese: へえ。ちょっと本格的だね。 聞いたことない調味料ばっかり。
English: Now I know I'm in a French restaurant. I've never heard of most of these seasonings.

Japanese: “オイスター・ソース”とか。 なるほどくん、知ってる？
English: Hey, Nick! This container has... oyster sauce? What's that?

Japanese: （それって、中華料理の 調味料じゃなかったっけ）
English: (Isn't that used in Chinese food...?)

Japanese: 包丁もぎらぎらに 研いであるしねー。
English: And look at these knives. They look really sharp!

Japanese: そんな包丁で、みそ汁の わかめ、切ってみたいな。
English: I'd like to see how one of those slices through a cheesecake.

Japanese: せっかくだし、もっと刃ごたえが あるモノを切ろうよ。
English: A cheesecake? You don't exactly need a sharp knife for one of those, Nick.

Japanese: この、宝のハコみたいなのは なんだろう。
English: What's this? It looks like a treasure chest or something.

Japanese: うわ、すごい！ 小さなビンがギッシリ。
English: Wow! Look at all these little bottles!

Japanese: アロマテラピーのオイルか。 床の上にまで、あふれてるぞ‥‥
English: Oh! They're aromatherapy oils! He's got so many, they're overflowing onto the floor...

Japanese: １００本はあるよ！ おんなじ小ビンが。
English: Let's see... 1, 2, 3... ...98, 99, 100! They're all the same, too.

Japanese: おっきなカガミがあるよ。
English: Now THIS is one large mirror!

Japanese: 店長さん、ここで おめかしするんだねー。
English: I bet this is where he makes himself look pretty.

Japanese: 机の上に、手作りの本があるな。 《クラリス・ホンドボーの寝室》。
English: There's a book on the dresser. "Claurice Armstrong's Bedtime Literature".

Japanese: いかがわしいタイトルだね。
English: Not exactly Pulitzer Prize material, is it?

Japanese: これは‥‥本土坊さんの 詩集みたいだな。
English: It looks like a collection of poems he's written.

Japanese: 詩‥‥？　いいねえ。 ちょっと読んでみてよ！
English: Poems? Cool! Read one out!

Japanese: ゆたかな感情をこめて おねがいね。
English: And say it in your best French accent... with intensity! OK?

Japanese: えーと‥‥じゃあ、これ。 タイトルは“ぷらんたん”。
English: OK, um... Here's one. Ahem! It's called, "Printemps".

Japanese: 『‥‥映画のような２人は 今まで気がつかなかったコーヒー
English: "Zee two of zem, like actors from a film. Zee coffee still undrunk.

Japanese: 早すぎるささやきは悲しみの日曜日 意味のない朝は心地よいカクテル
English: "Sweet nothings over too soon, on zat sad Sunday morning. Zee foolish cocktail so delicious.

Japanese: あなたが紅茶を飲みほすのなら 私、ウソをついちゃう‥‥きっと』
English: "Take zee last sip of your tea and I know what I will do. I must lie to you. I must."

Japanese: ‥‥え？　なに？　おわり？
English: Huh? That's it!?

Japanese: ‥‥ま、まあ、 こんなもんだよ。詩は。
English: Yup. That's a poem for you.

Japanese: お。いいニオイ。 ナベでぐつぐつ言ってるのは‥‥
English: Mmm. That smells good. Something's bubbling away nicely in that pot.

Japanese: オマールえびとアワビのフリカッセ バルサミコ酢風味だね。
English: It must be the lobster and abalone fricassée with a balsamic vinaigrette.

Japanese: きのうのランチじゃないか‥‥
English: Isn't that what I ate yesterday for lunch?

Japanese: すっかりアタマに 焼きついちゃったんだもん。
English: Yeah, I think it's been permanently burned into my brain now...

Japanese: マズい料理の代表選手として。
English: ...as an example of what truly awful food tastes like.

Japanese: （ホンモノはウマいだろうに。 ‥‥バルサミコ酢風味）
English: (I bet it'd taste really good if it was prepared well. ...Balsamy even!)

Japanese: ‥‥このレースのカーテンは なんなんだろう。
English: What are these lace curtains for?

Japanese: でも、なんかこう、 いいムードだねえ。
English: I don't know. But they give the place a real homey feel, don't they?

Japanese: こんなところに ぶら下がれるんなら、
English: Hmm... Lace curtains...

Japanese: あたし、ナベになっても いいかも。
English: You know, if I was a cooking pot, I'd be perfectly happy to sit on a shelf under those.

Japanese: （‥‥ぼくとしては なんと答えればいいんだ‥‥？）
English: (How do you respond to something like that!?)

Japanese: それにしても‥‥お客さんが 来ませんね。このレストラン。
English: You don't exactly have many customers, do you, Mr. Armstrong?

Japanese: オー。そおねえ。 ちょうどいいんじゃないかしら。
English: Non, you are right, monsieur. But per'aps z'at is la perfect time for you to visit me, non?

Japanese: お客サマには、ゆったりと 食事してほしいから。
English: Now I can give you my undivided attention, and cook for you la dish suprême!

Japanese: ‥‥強がり、言ってるぞ。
English: Putting on a brave face, huh?

Japanese: ううん、女ゴコロだねえ。
English: That's what girls do, Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥でも。おかげでお店は 苦しくなるばかり。
English: But you are right. Business is very difficult z'ese days.

Japanese: やっぱり、吐麗美庵の“吐”の イメージがワルかったのかしら。
English: Per'aps ze name is la problem. People do not understand it. Z'ey think it is "tray".

Japanese: “吐息がもれるほどオイシイ”って いう意味だったのに‥‥。
English: I just wanted people to think z'at my restaurant was exclusive.

Japanese: ‥‥グチ、こぼしだしたぞ。
English: But they think you just serve fast food on cheap, plastic trays?

Japanese: ううん、めめしいねえ。
English: Nick! That's the kind of thing that can make a girl cry!

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃん。本土坊さんが オトコだって、忘れかけてない？
English: Have you forgotten that Mr. Armstrong is a man, Maya?

Japanese: ‥‥でも。このお店は ワタシのすべてだから。
English: But z'is restaurant is my life! It is everything to me!

Japanese: なんとしても！　守りぬいて 見せちゃうんだから！
English: I will defend it to la finale! No one will take it from me!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。ダレなんですか？ さっき帰っていった、女の人は。
English: So, who was that woman you were just talking to?

Japanese: オー・ラ・ラ‥‥見てたのかしら？
English: Oh là là! You saw z'at?

Japanese: え、ええ。すみません。
English: Ah, well... Yes. Sorry.

Japanese: なんかこう、 しとやかなヒトでしたよねー。
English: So? Who was she!? She looked so polite and graceful.

Japanese: え！　シ、シトヤカ‥‥？
English: P-Polite!? G-Graceful!?

Japanese: おさしみと火事が大好き、とか。
English: And she likes raw meat and fires, right?

Japanese: ‥‥来月、また来ますから‥‥
English: I'll be back next month.

Japanese: おま、おま、おま、 お待ちしてるわあ！
English: Oui! Naturellement! I will be waiting for you!

Japanese: ‥‥今度いただけなかったら‥‥ つけますね‥‥火‥‥
English: If you haven't got it by then, I'm afraid it might get a little... hot... around here.

Japanese: い、いやだワ！ ちゃんと用意するから！
English: Non! I will 'ave everything ready, I promise!

Japanese: （‥‥このへんの会話を 考えれば‥‥）
English: (Now that I think about it...)

Japanese: （何のハナシをしていたのか、 なんとなく、わかる気がするな）
English: Hey, Maya... I think it's pretty clear what kind of conversation they were having.

Japanese: じゃあ。じゃあ。その証拠品、 見せてみようよ！
English: You think so? Well, then let's show him that piece of evidence and see what happens!

Japanese: さっき帰っていった女の人は、 やっぱり‥‥？
English: The woman who was here earlier... I take it that she's... um...

Japanese: ‥‥はああ。まったく、どうして こんなコトになったのかしら‥‥
English: Why 'as it come to z'is...? What a tragedy!

Japanese: 気がついたら、借金まみれ。 社会がワルいと思うんだけど。
English: Suddenly I find myself so deep in la debt. It is a sign of la bad, bad world we live in, uh?

Japanese: （‥‥料理のウデが ワルいんじゃないかな‥‥）
English: (No, I'd say it's more of a sign of la bad, bad culinary skills...)

Japanese: あの気味のワルいオンナはね。 お察しのとおり、ローン会社の子。
English: Z'e woman who was 'ere... Z'e scary woman... She is from la loan office.

Japanese: ローン会社‥‥５０００万円を 借りているところですか？
English: Loan office? Is that where you borrowed half a million dollars from?

Japanese: え、ええ‥‥。 《カリヨーゼ》っていうんだけど。
English: Oui. Tender Lender, it is called.

Japanese: なんか、カルいキモチで 借りちゃいたくなる名前ですね。
English: Catchy name. Just hearing it makes me wanna borrow some money.

Japanese: あそこで借りるなら、 １０００円までにしときなさい！
English: Please! You must not borrow from z'em. If you must, no more z'an ten dollars!

Japanese: （そんなの、真宵ちゃんの おこづかいじゃないか‥‥）
English: Ten dollars? Sounds like your whole monthly stipend, Maya...

Japanese: いくらなんでも もう少しあるよ！
English: Hey! I get a bit more than that, thank you very much!

Japanese: 本土坊さんが５０００万円の 借金をしている会社、か‥‥。
English: So Tender Lender is the loan office you borrowed half a million from, huh?

Japanese: 事件に関係あるのかな？
English: I wonder if they've got anything to do with this case...

Japanese: ワタシ‥‥ナミダでキレイになる タイプのオンナなのね。
English: I am a weak woman. When I am upset, I 'ave to buy something nice to cheer me up.

Japanese: アイツのおかげで、借金は ５０００万円にふくれあがって、
English: Thanks to 'im loaning me la money, I 'ave to pay back 'alf a million dollars now!

Japanese: もう、ナニを言われても 逆らえないわ‥‥アイツに。
English: I am like 'is slave. I 'ave to do everything z'at 'e tells me.

Japanese: あの。“アイツ”‥‥って、 どなたですか？
English: Um... Who's this he?

Japanese: 《ナニワのゼニトラ》‥‥。
English: La Tiger...

Japanese: なにわ‥‥
English: The Tiger?

Japanese: 《カリヨーゼ》の社長サン。 そりゃもう、コワいのよお。
English: Oui. 'E is la manager of z'e Tender Lender. A terrifying man! La big city mobster!

Japanese: 大阪弁でドナりつけられると、 ３日ぐらい耳鳴りがやまなくて。
English: When 'e shouts at me, my knees are trembling and 'is voice is ringing in my ears for 3 days!

Japanese: オンボロのスクーターの音が聞こえ ただけで、ワタシ、泣いちゃうの。
English: As soon as I 'ear la noise of z'at battered old scooter 'e rides, I start to cry!

Japanese: （“大阪弁”に“オンボロの スクーター”‥‥？）
English: (A big city mobster who rides a battered old scooter...?)

Japanese: ‥‥もしかして、その男‥‥ ぼくに似てますか？
English: Um, does this guy resemble me by any chance?

Japanese: オオ‥‥ノンノンゼンゼン。 もお、ハクリョクがちがうわ。
English: Oh, non, non, non! Z'is man 'as a presence! A most formidable personality!

Japanese: ‥‥あ。でも、そうねえ‥‥ そのトガったアタマは‥‥
English: Although... Oui, 'e does 'ave la spikey 'air just like you.

Japanese: ちょっと、似てるかも。
English: Oui, z'ere is a resemblance z'ere, I suppose.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥）
（行ってみたほうがよさそうだな。 そのローン会社‥‥）
English: (Sounds like this loan office is worth checking out after all...)

Japanese: 《カリヨーゼ》なら、 ビタミン広場の先にあるわあ。
English: If you want to visit z'e Tender Lender, it is just beyond Vitamin Square.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Hey, Nick...

Japanese: おカネなら、あたしが貸すよ？ ３００円までなら。
English: If you need money, I can loan you some... As long as it's less than 3 dollars...

Japanese: （ビタミン広場の先、か‥‥）
English: Um... Thanks for the offer... (...Just beyond Vitamin Square, huh?)

Japanese: モン・デュ！　ステキな マドモワゼルね‥‥！
English: Mon dieu! What beauty!

Japanese: あ！　あたしの お姉ちゃんなんですよ！
English: That's my sister!

Japanese: あらあら。セ・ラ・ヴィ！ そおねえ‥‥
English: Ah, oui? Another delight!

Japanese: アナタにも、お姉さんには ないミリョクが、きっとあるわ。
English: But you 'ave a certain je ne sais quois z'at I do not sense from your sister.

Japanese: は、はあ。
English: I do?

Japanese: だから、ムネを張って 生きていきなさいな。
English: So throw your 'ead 'igh! Thrust out your feminine features!

Japanese: くじけちゃダメよ！ オーエス！　オーエス！
English: Do not lose 'eart, ma fille! You are a woman! A woman extraordinaire!

Japanese: ‥‥なんで、なぐさめられなきゃ ならないんだろ。
English: Do I look like I need cheering up or something?

Japanese: ‥‥オー・ラ・ラ。アナタ、 とってもいいエガオじゃない！
English: Oh là là! You 'ave such a pretty smile, my petite chulip!

Japanese: え！　そうですか？
English: Who, me?

Japanese: やっぱり、アタシたち女性は エガオがダイジよねえ！
English: Oui. La smile is the most important thing for us girls, n'est-ce pas?

Japanese: そ‥‥そうですよね。
English: Um... Sure!

Japanese: ウエイトレス向きのカオだと 思うんだけどねえ‥‥。
English: You 'ave la perfect face for a waitress, you know.

Japanese: ‥‥すみません。 ユメにやぶれたら、また来ます。
English: Um, thanks. I guess if things don't work out someday, then maybe I'll be back.

Japanese: （ユメ‥‥って、霊媒師か？）
English: (What things? Is she talking about her being a spirit medium...?)

Japanese: そのカオを見せられると、 とってもツラいわあ。
English: Oh, 'ow it 'urts me to see z'at poor girl's face!

Japanese: マコちゃんですか‥‥？
English: Maggey's?

Japanese: 超人的に不幸な子だって 聞いてたけど‥‥
English: I 'eard she was super'umanly un'appy.

Japanese: ちょ、ちょうじん‥‥
English: Superhumanly...?

Japanese: ウチの店に来なければ、 こんな目にはあわなかったわ。
English: And if she 'adn't come to work for me, she wouldn't 'ave got into z'is mess.
(Huh? I'm missing something here, aren't I?)

Japanese: ジュ・スイ・デゾレ‥‥ ゴメンなさいね‥‥マコちゃん。
English: Je suis désolé, Maggey! Forgive me!

Japanese: ‥‥亡くなったカタね。 初めてのお客さんだったわ。
English: Ah, la dead man. La first in my restaurant.

Japanese: え！　記念すべき お客さん第１号‥‥
English: No way! You mean, the first customer you ever had!?

Japanese: そうじゃなくて‥‥
English: Non, non, non.

Japanese: 吐麗美庵に来たのが 初めてだったの。
English: It was z'e first time I've 'ad a dead man in my restaurant.

Japanese: ‥‥オキノドクね。
English: Such a s'ame to lose a customer after only 'is first meal 'ere!

Japanese: あら。このオジさんなら 常連さんよォ。
English: Ah, z'is old man. 'E is a regular 'ere.

Japanese: ウチのジマンのコーヒーと お冷やが大好きなの。
English: My specialty of cappuccino and cold water is one of 'is favorites.

Japanese: あの事件が起こったときも いたんですよね？
English: Was he here at the time of the incident?

Japanese: そうだったわねェ。 たしかに、いたわ。
English: Oui. 'E was 'ere. I am sure of it.

Japanese: あの事件が起こったときに、ね‥‥
English: When la incident 'appened, z'e old man was 'ere.

Japanese: こう見えても、パリのお店で ５年間、みっちり修行したのよォ。
English: It is 'ard to imagine, I know, but I was an apprentice for five years in la gay Paris.

Japanese: へええ。スゴいですねー！
English: Wow! In Paris!?

Japanese: そのお店のシェフのヒトコトで、 自分のお店を開く決心をしたの。
English: It was something la chef z'ere said to me z'at made me decide to open my own restaurant.

Japanese: なんて言われたんですか？
English: What was it?

Japanese: 『あと１０年修行が必要だ』って。
English: Z'at awful man said to me, "You must train for another ten years!"

Japanese: やってられないでしょ？　だから 帰ってきちゃったのよねえ。
English: Oh! It was such a shock! So unexpected! But you would 'ave to be a fool to do z'is, uh?

Japanese: これなんですけど‥‥
English: Um, about this...

Japanese: フェリシタシオン！ メ‥‥ケスク・セ？
English: Felicitations! Mais... qu'est-ce que c'est?

Japanese: え‥‥！ み‥‥、みー・とぅー。
English: Ack! Um... Je... Je no comprehend...

Japanese: なるほどくん。 テキトーに返事しない！
English: Nick! Don't just make something up!

Japanese: それがあるかぎり、ワタシ、 ツバサの折れたエンジェルなのね。
English: So long as z'at paper exists, I am but a delightful angel w'iz la broken wings!

Japanese: えんじぇる‥‥
English: An angel, huh...? Doesn't bode well, when you think about it...

Japanese: 毎月まいつき、ギュウギュウに シボりあげられた上‥‥、
English: Oui! Z'ey kept 'arassing me month after month.

Japanese: ‥‥ついには、あんなコトまで 手伝わされちゃって‥‥
English: In z'e end, I 'ad to give in. I agreed to 'elp z'em.

Japanese: “あんなコト”‥‥？
English: Help them? With what?

Japanese: あ！　借金がなければ、 モチロン断ったわよ！
English: Mais bien sûr, if I did not owe z'em la money, I would 'ave refused.

Japanese: でも‥‥しかたないじゃないの！
English: But my 'ands were tied!

Japanese: いったい、ナニを 手伝わされたんですか？
English: Please! What did you agree to help them with?

Japanese: そ‥‥それだけは言えないわ！
English: Non. I-I cannot say!

Japanese: 言っちゃったら、あのオンナに おさしみにされて‥‥
English: If I tell you, z'at woman, she will slice me up...

Japanese: ワサビじょうゆで食べられちゃう！
English: ...and eat me w'iz la salad garnish!

Japanese: （“おさしみにされちゃう” ‥‥か）
English: (Eww! I hope he doesn't mean that he'll literally be sliced up and served with garnish...)

Japanese: どうやら‥‥さっきの女の人、 借用書と関係があるようですね。
English: I'm going to guess that woman has something to do with your loan contract. Am I right?
Ah!

Japanese: あの女の人のコト‥‥ 聞かせてくださいっ！
English: Please, Mr. Armstrong, tell us about that woman!

Japanese: まあ、キレイ！ イヤリングにピッタリね！
English: Oh, what a beautiful pendant! It would look stunning on me w'iz la earrings!

Japanese: もう、とらないでくださいね！ １個しかありませんから。
English: Please don't steal it again, OK? It's one of a kind!

Japanese: うすくスライスすれば いいじゃない！
English: I could just 'ave a sliver of it, non?

Japanese: ち、チーズみたいに 言わないでください！
English: I-It's not a block of cheese, you know!

Japanese: この新聞、被害者が 持ってきたものなんですよ。
English: This is the paper the victim brought with him.

Japanese: 《クリーニング・ボンバー》 ‥‥ねえ。
English: "MC Bomber"...? Oui...

Japanese: 心当たり、ありますか？
English: Does that mean something to you?

Japanese: さあ‥‥ワタシ、フランス語しか わからないから。
English: Non, I only speak French, mademoiselle.

Japanese: マガジンラックのウラに 落ちていたんですけど‥‥
English: But we found it behind your magazine rack...

Japanese: あらあら。きっと、 捨て忘れてたのね。
English: Oh, 'ow naughty I am! I 'aven't cleaned for such a long time!

Japanese: オー・ラ・ラ！
English: Oh là là! So you 'ave eaten my lunch, oui?

Japanese: いかがだったかしら？ ウチのランチ。
English: Tell me, monsieur. Did you enjoy la lunch I prepared for you?

Japanese: ‥‥個性的で、野性的な味でした。
English: It was a... unique and... wild mixture of flavors.

Japanese: オマールえび、って あたし、初めて食べました！
English: That was the first time I ever had lobster, you know!

Japanese: オマールえび‥‥？ そんなの、使ってないケド‥‥
English: Lobster? Z'ere was no lobster in my lunch...

Japanese: え！　だって、メニューに そう書いてありましたよ。
English: Huh? But, it said, "lobster" right on the menu.

Japanese: マドモワゼル・マヨイ。 メニューはちゃんと読んで。
English: Mademoiselle Maya. Please, you must read la menu more carefully.

Japanese: オマールえびとアワビのフリカッセ バルサミコ酢風味の、“何か”。
English: It says, "A dish inspired by lobster and abalone fricassée with a balsamic vinaigrette."

Japanese: な、“何か”‥‥ですかあ。
English: Oh. It's "inspired", is it?

Japanese: 最後に“風味”とつけてる ところがミソなのよお。
English: Oui. "Inspired" by z'ese ingredients, but not per'aps made from z'em, n'est-ce pas?

Japanese: こりゃ、いっぱい食わされたね、 なるほどくん。
English: Well, guess that's the way the cookie crumbles, Nick.

Japanese: （‥‥いったい、 ナニを食わされたんだ！）
English: (The way the lunch tasted, it's probably 99% perspiration and 1% inspiration.)

Japanese: ど、どうかしましたか？
English: What is it?

Japanese: そのスクーター‥‥ あなたのかしら？
English: Is z'is your scooter?

Japanese: い、いえ。ビタミン広場で 見かけたんですけど‥‥
English: No, no! I saw it before in Vitamin Square, you see...

Japanese: オオ、モン・デュ！ そんなの、見せないでちょうだい！
English: Mon dieu! Non, non, non! Take it away! Do not show z'at to me!

Japanese: （なんだ、この反応は‥‥？）
English: (Talk about an allergic reaction...)

Japanese: ‥‥あのとき、トナリの クジに手を出していたら‥‥
English: If only I 'ad taken z'e right lottery ticket...

Japanese: 今ごろ、借金とも お別れだったのにねえ。
English: I would not 'ave any debts right now. I would be free!

Japanese: いいじゃないですか。 １００円、当たったんですから！
English: Look on the bright side, Mr. Armstrong. You won a dollar, right?

Japanese: （‥‥あんまり反省してない みたいだな‥‥）
English: (A lack of remorse for having stolen something: Priceless.)

Japanese: たしかに、よく割れるのよお、 ワタシの花ビン。
English: Ah, it is true. My vases are broken all z'e time.

Japanese: 事件があった次の日に、 新しいのを買ってきたんだけど、
English: I bought a new one z'e next day after la incident.

Japanese: “捜査中だから”って言われて、 置かせてくれなくて。
English: But z'ey wouldn't let me put it on la table because of la investigation.

Japanese: そんなの見せなくても、 だいじょうぶよお。
English: Please, monsieur. Z'ere is no need to show me z'at.

Japanese: ムッシュ・ナルホドーでしょ。 ‥‥ウデの悪い弁護士さん。
English: You are Mon. Phoenix Wright, z'e worst defense lawyer in town.

Japanese: （しっかり、まちがって インプットされているな‥‥）
English: (I'm pretty sure I know how he formed this completely wrong impression of me...)

Japanese: あの‥‥前に会ったときも、 このバッジ、つけてました？
English: Um, the last time we met, did I show you this badge?

Japanese: ウイ。こないだも 見せてたでしょ、それ。
English: Oui. You flashed it to everyone in la restaurant!

Japanese: ニセモノも、それを見せるのが 好きみたいだね。
English: Looks like Xin Eohp is a bigger fan of flashing stuff than you are, Nick!

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 吐麗美庵　キッチン
English: January 7 Trés Bien Kitchen

Japanese: あ、あれ。 またいないよ、店長さん。
English: Looks like Mr. Armstrong's out again.

Japanese: ‥‥開店中のレストランで、 シェフが“いない”って‥‥
English: But the place is open for business...

Japanese: どう考えても あり得ないぞ、ふつう！
English: You can't have an open restaurant without a chef!

Japanese: あ、あたしに怒られても‥‥。
English: Hey! It's not my fault, Nick! Don't take it out on me!

Japanese: （事件が起こってからの、 数分間‥‥）
English: (Only a couple of minutes after the incident happened...)

Japanese: （五十嵐さんは店をはなれ、 本土坊さんは１人きりだった）
English: (Mr. Kudo left the scene, leaving Mr. Armstrong here alone.)

Japanese: ううん‥‥ハナシを 聞きたいのにねえ。
English: Argh... Missing when we need to talk to him the most.

Japanese: （もしかして‥‥ぼくたちから 逃げてるのか？）
English: (Maybe he's trying to avoid us on purpose?)

Japanese: （今はまだ、証拠が 足りないのかもしれない）
English: (I don't think I have enough evidence yet...)

Japanese: （もう少し調査をして、 材料を集めてみるか‥‥）
English: (I should investigate and gather some more clues before I try again...)

Japanese: ‥なるほどくん‥
English: ...Mr. Nick...

Japanese: ‥これ以上つづけると、 ココロがこわれてしまいます‥
English: ...If you push yourself any more, your soul will shatter...

Japanese: ‥もう一度、冷静になって 考えなおしましょう‥
English: ...Please calm down, collect your thoughts, and try again...

Japanese: （‥‥しまった！ シッパイしすぎたか‥‥）
English: (Nnrgh! I've made too many mistakes!)
